title: Police Identification and Records Officers
description: Collect evidence at crime scene, classify and identify fingerprints, and photograph evidence for use in criminal and civil cases.

tasks.text: Photograph crime or accident scenes for evidence records.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Maintain records of evidence and write and review reports.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Submit evidence to supervisors, crime labs, or court officials for legal proceedings.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Testify in court and present evidence.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Look for trace evidence, such as fingerprints, hairs, fibers, or shoe impressions, using alternative light sources when necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Dust selected areas of crime scene and lift latent fingerprints, adhering to proper preservation procedures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Analyze and process evidence at crime scenes, during autopsies, or in the laboratory, wearing protective equipment and using powders and chemicals.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Process film and prints from crime or accident scenes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Serve as technical advisor and coordinate with other law enforcement workers or legal personnel to exchange information on crime scene collection activities.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Create sketches and diagrams, by hand or computer software, to depict crime scenes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Coordinate or conduct instructional classes or in-services, such as citizen police academy classes and crime scene training for other officers.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Identify, compare, classify, and file fingerprints, using systems such as Automated Fingerprint Identification System (AFIS) or the Henry Classification System.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Interview survivors, witnesses, suspects, and other law enforcement personnel.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
